headline: KEY Q1 Deep Dive: Commercial Lending and Fee Businesses Drive Strong Start to 2026
summary: Regional banking company KeyCorp (NYSE:KEY) reported Q1 CY2026 results topping the market’s revenue expectations, with sales up 10.2% year on year to $1.95 billion. Its non-GAAP profit of $0.44 per share was 8% above analysts’ consensus estimates.

headline: FNB Q1 Earnings Meet Estimates, Revenues & Expenses Rise Y/Y
summary: F.N.B. Corp's Q1 earnings meet estimates, as higher NII and loan growth lift revenues, though rising expenses and provisions temper the overall performance.

headline: Scotiabank Comments on Expected Contribution from KeyCorp's First Quarter Earnings
summary: Scotiabank announced today that the expected net income contribution from its ownership interest in KeyCorp will be approximately CAD $77 million in Q2 2026. This contribution represents the Bank's share of KeyCorp's Q1 2026 net income, includes acquisition-related and other accounting impacts, is net of the Bank's associated funding costs and is reported on a one-month lag.

headline: KBW's Chris McGratty shares his take on multiple bank stocks
summary: Chris McGratty, KBW head of U.S. bank research, joins 'Squawk Box' to discuss his take on earnings from the banking sector, how he would position banks in a portfolio, his outlook for select bank stocks, and more.

headline: KeyCorp (KEY) Q1 2026 Earnings Call Highlights: Strong EPS Growth and Strategic Stock Repurchase
summary: KeyCorp (KEY) reports a robust first quarter with a 33% increase in EPS and a significant stock buyback, despite challenges in investment banking fees and deposit trends.
